title: AI Lab Journey
subtitle: Telkom University AI Laboratory. Study Group Member in 2024, Study Group Coordinator and Focused Group Member in 2025, Focused Group Coordinator from 2026.
reflection: Teaching weekly required understanding each concept well enough to handle any question on the spot.
Recruiting taught me what matters in a research context: track record, not just potential.
A coordinator's responsibilities are different from a member's. Members execute. Coordinators ensure others can execute consistently.
Supervising project groups showed me how many ways a project can be technically functional and scientifically weak at the same time.
Evaluating research quality means asking whether the question was worth answering, not just whether the code ran.

highlights: Joined as a first-year member and was assigned team lead on the plant disease classifier, the first leadership role in AI Lab.
Built the full pipeline from scratch: data cleaning, model training for computer vision, and Streamlit deployment. Delivered in one semester.
Won 3rd place at CCI Summit Kaggle, the first competition as team lead.
Awarded 2nd Best Final Project in the Study Group.

milestones.role: Study Group Coordinator · Focused Group Member
highlights: Managed 29 students through the full Study Group cycle, from recruitment to final project evaluation, by running weekly teaching sessions and all assessments.
Supervised 15 project groups end to end, from proposal through final demonstration, providing guidance throughout and serving as evaluator at every stage.
Led the Focused Group deepfake detection project: one model identifying both video and audio manipulation, with output explaining each decision.
Appointed Focused Group Coordinator, overseeing the research-active tier within AI Lab.
Setting research direction, reviewing member output, and connecting projects to industry and academic partners.
Three concurrent research tracks: medical imaging, NLP, and computer vision.
